title: Collectiv Gallery
description: A center for innovative art, offering a dynamic space for both emerging and established artists.
thingsLearned: Working on this project deepened my understanding of spatial storytelling and how digital interfaces can evoke the feeling of physical gallery spaces. I explored how negative space and typography weight can guide a viewer's eye just as lighting does in a real exhibition.
approach: I started by visiting three local galleries and documenting their wayfinding systems. The design emerged from those observations — a restrained layout that lets the artwork breathe, with navigation that feels more like a gallery map than a website menu.

title: Photography Archive
description: An archival platform for documentary photographers to share and preserve their long-form visual essays.
thingsLearned: This project taught me a great deal about image optimization pipelines and how to maintain color fidelity across different screen calibrations. I also learned how metadata-driven layouts can adapt gracefully without losing editorial control.
approach: The core challenge was building a system flexible enough for hundreds of different photo series while keeping each one feeling individually curated. I developed a JSON schema that gives photographers control over pacing, sequencing, and text placement.

title: Residency Program
description: A six-week artist residency connecting practitioners across disciplines to collaborate on site-specific works.
thingsLearned: Facilitating cross-disciplinary collaboration revealed how different creative fields have entirely different notions of process, deadline, and output. Designing systems that accommodate all of these required deep listening before any planning.
approach: Rather than imposing a framework, I spent the first two weeks conducting interviews and mapping each artist's workflow. The resulting structure was built around what already existed, not what I assumed would work.
